title: Hedging and tentative language
explanation:
Hedging and tentative language

Hedging softens claims in academic and professional English.

  • It would appear that...
  • There is some evidence to suggest...
  • This may well indicate...
  • Tends to, appears to, seems to

questions.text: It [*would appear|appears definitely|must appear] that consumer habits are changing.
There is [*some|no|total] evidence to suggest a link between sleep and memory.
The results [*may well|must certainly|will never] indicate a broader trend.
Urban populations [*tend to|always|never] grow faster than rural ones.
This [*could arguably|definitely must|clearly cannot] be seen as a turning point.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The policy ____ to have reduced emissions slightly. | *appears | proves | guarantees
It is ____ possible that the figures were incomplete. | *entirely | somewhat | absolutely
The author ____ that further study is required. | *implies | proclaims | orders
These findings should be interpreted with ____. | *caution | certainty | anger
It [*seems reasonable to assume|is proven absolutely|is impossible to assume] that costs will rise.
The data [*suggests rather than proves|proves without doubt|denies completely] a connection.
One [*might argue|must argue|cannot argue] that the law needs reform.
The trend is [*likely to|definitely going to|never going to] continue.
It is [*not entirely clear|completely obvious|absolutely certain] why sales fell.
